sections.paragraph: m. lofty place, summit, from the root טָבַר i.q. צָבַר. Jud. 9:37 , יֹרְדִים מֵעִם טַבּוּר הָאָרֶץ (verse 36 Jdg 9:36, רָאשֵׁי הֶהָרִים) “they come down from the height of the land.” Eze 38:12, ישְׁבֵי עַל־טַבּוּר הָאָרֶץ “who dwell in the height of the earth,” i.e. the holy land; which the Hebrews considered to be more lofty than other countries; comp. הָרֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל Eze 6:2 33:28 35:12 38:8 . To this correspond Sam. Act 8:1-40 Eth. ደብር፡ a mountain. LXX., Vulg., translate טַבּוּר umbilicus, as though it were the summit of the belly. Compare Talmud טִיבוּר the navel.
